Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: focus on the XOR operator that chooses between checking local affiliation D and skipping that step

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: if applicant is local resident and member of a known community group they get a subtle score uplift otherwise the step is skipped

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This gives advantage to those who are part of that group which may be correlated with race ethnicity or other protected characteristics

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Even if not legally protected it can still create disparate impact

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Need to discuss fairness metrics demographic parity equal opportunity and related fairness concerns

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: giving incremental advantage may lead to unfairness even if not overtly discriminatory and can lead to systemic inequality

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: potential legal implications under anti discrimination laws and disparate impact analysis should be considered

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: there is potential for unintentional bias due to correlation between community membership and socioeconomic status

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: it can reinforce existing inequalities by rewarding those already privileged

Name: Validation - PE
Text: stakeholders should understand why some applicants get uplift and the model should be audited for disparate impact

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: mitigation strategies include removing D from decision path or making it mandatory for all using objective criteria and incorporating fairness constraints
